commit.message:
feat: add multi-colony support with per-colony pheromones and new instructions

- Support 1-4 colonies competing/cooperating on the same map
- Each colony runs its own ant-ssembly program independently
- Per-colony pheromone layers (3D array) with unique signatures
- New instructions: COLONY, SNIFF_OTHER, SMELL_OTHER, MIMIC
- Existing EMIT/SNIFF/SMELL/DROP/AT_HOME are now colony-aware
- Colony class with per-colony program, ants, and color config
- All 6 maps support num_colonies param with corner-based homes
- Frontend: colony selector, per-colony colors, per-colony stats
- API: init accepts colonies array, load_program/set_ants accept colony_id
- 72 tests passing (12 new multi-colony tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Create benchmarks comparing Kabs estimates with OpenPNM (#25)

* Update dependencies to include openpnm version constraints in pyproject.toml and pixi.toml

* Add benchmark for segmented volume comparison with OpenPNM

* Add synthetic porous-volume benchmark notebook against OpenPNM

This notebook generates synthetic spanning porous volumes with voids, converts them into grayscale observations, segments them, extracts pore networks using snow2, and compares permeability estimates between voids and OpenPNM. It includes detailed markdown explanations, case specifications, and visualizations for representative cases and comparison plots.

* Update README

* Add tests for binary input validation in segmented volume benchmark

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: redesign release pipeline to create releases atomically with assets

- Remove 'draft: true' from release-please config (draft releases don't
  trigger events and cause duplicate PRs)
- Add 'skip-github-release: true' so Release Please only creates the tag
- Release Please outputs tag_name and calls release.yml via workflow_call
- release.yml builds macOS/Windows assets then creates the GitHub Release
  with all assets attached in a single atomic operation
- Publish step extracts changelog from CHANGELOG.md for release notes
- Cleans up any leftover draft releases before creating the final one

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Browser +8: Service Worker lifecycle (register/unregister/list/update),
Cache Storage (list/clear), push notification sim, offline mode

Agent +10: WCAG audit, ARIA tree, contrast check, landmark nav,
focus order, alt text audit, heading structure, role validate,
keyboard trap detect, screen reader simulation

Stealth +7: Human behavior simulation — random delays, Bézier mouse
curves, natural typing with typos, human scroll, behavior profiles,
stealth_max (all patches combined), stealth scoring

SSE listen/messages, GraphQL subscription

commit.message:
fix(terrain): bilinear interpolation in getHeightAt, spawn offset 100->5m

getHeightAt now matches GPU shader (LinearFilter = bilinear interp).
Floor-only sampling caused char to float above slopes by several meters.
Spawn offset reduced from 100m to 5m (correct heights now confirmed).

Add procedural variation: seeded path perturbation per element

Each element instance gets a variationSeed on placement. During
print rendering, SVG path coordinates are slightly perturbed using
a seeded PRNG (mulberry32), so the same element placed twice looks
subtly different. Amount scales with carve level (0.6/0.9/1.3px).

feat(warp): fail fast on first target compilation error (#37510)

Stop building remaining targets when the first target fails
type-checking.

Sequential path: break out of the compilation loop on first failure.
Parallel path: add isFailure predicate to executeTaskGraph to prevent
launching new tasks after a failure is detected; in-flight tasks are
allowed to finish.

Both paths now filter partial results so callers only see targets that
were actually compiled.

commit.message:
Add model thinking/effort mode support (#81)

* chore: scaffold branch for #38

* feat: add --effort flag for AI reasoning effort control

Add a unified `--effort <level>` CLI flag and `.wade.yml` config option
that maps to each AI tool's native reasoning effort mechanism:

- Claude: `--settings '{"effortLevel": "<level>"}'`
- Codex: `-c model_reasoning_effort="<mapped>"` (max → xhigh)
- Cursor: model variant resolution (appends `-thinking` for high/max)
- OpenCode: `--variant <level>` (max → high)
- Copilot/Gemini/VSCode/Antigravity: graceful no-op

Resolution chain: CLI --effort → ai.<command>.effort → ai.effort → None

Changes:
- Add EffortLevel enum (low/medium/high/max) and supports_effort capability
- Add effort fields to AICommandConfig, AIConfig, and ProjectConfig
- Add effort_args() and resolve_effort_model() to AbstractAITool base
- Implement effort in Claude, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode adapters
- Add resolve_effort() service with full fallback chain
- Update confirm_ai_selection() to 3-tuple (tool, model, effort)
- Add --effort to plan-task, implement-task, batch, and deps commands
- Add effort prompt to `wade init` flow
- Add 57 unit tests covering all effort functionality

Closes #38

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: update review_service test mock for 3-tuple confirm_ai_selection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review comments — effort sentinel and stale-effort on tool switch

- init_service: return "" sentinel when user picks "(none — use tool
  default)" at effort prompt so _patch_config can distinguish an
  explicit clear from no prompt shown
- init_service._patch_config: when default_effort is "" and force=True,
  delete ai["effort"] instead of leaving stale value in place
- ai_resolution: clear stale effort when user switches to a tool that
  does not support effort (confirm_ai_selection Change AI tool path)
- tests: add TestChangeEffort with two regression tests covering the
  Change effort menu path and the stale-effort-on-tool-switch bug

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: notCharles <charles@pelican.dev>

stability: add docstrings, frontend static serving, and K8s API timeouts

- Add complete module/class/function docstrings to all Python files in
  backend/ and ai-engine/ (main, database, utils, models, services,
  controllers, repositories, router, analyzers)

- Backend now conditionally serves the React SPA from frontend/build/
  when the directory is present (K8s single-pod / local dev mode):
  * Mounts frontend/build/static at /static via StaticFiles
  * Adds /{full_path:path} catch-all that returns index.html for any
    non-API, non-static route, enabling client-side React Router
  * Root GET / keeps its JSON health-check response for backwards compat
  * FRONTEND_BUILD_DIR env var overrides the default build path

- Add explicit _request_timeout parameters to all Kubernetes Python
  client calls in pod_service.py and the cluster-status endpoint:
  * List calls: 30 s  (pod listing, event listing)
  * Read calls: 15 s  (single pod object)
  * Log calls:  20 s  (pod log streaming)
  * Cluster status probe: 10 s
  Prevents indefinite hangs when the K8s API server is unreachable or
  slow inside the cluster

All 28 existing tests continue to pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(deps): bump `slotmap` minimum version to `1.1.0` (#2640)

`slotmap` introduced `const fn from_ffi` in `1.1.0`, but all three
crates were pinned to `1.0.0`, causing compilation failures when that
API is used.

## Changes
- Bumped `slotmap` from `1.0.0` → `1.1.0` in:
  - `dfir_rs/Cargo.toml`
  - `dfir_lang/Cargo.toml`
  - `hydro_lang/Cargo.toml`

The resolved version in `Cargo.lock` is already `1.1.1`, so no lock file
churn is expected.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: MingweiSamuel <6778341+MingweiSamuel@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement P0 integrity policy gate (#29)

* feat: implement P0 integrity policy gate (#12)

Introduce `IntegrityPolicyGate` as the explicit pre-persistence gate
that enforces route identity, signature, sequence, and prev-hash checks
in a single named abstraction before any record is allowed through to
storage. `IngestService` now delegates all integrity enforcement to the
gate, with the payload-hash pre-check remaining in the service layer.

Add three acceptance-criteria integration tests via `IngestService`:
- rejects_tampered_signature_via_ingest_service
- rejects_replay_attempt_via_ingest_service
- rejects_out_of_order_sequence_via_ingest_service

Update AGENTS.md (mandatory test rule, stale IngestState ref),
CONCEPTS.md (section 6), and copilot-instructions.md (ingest/ layout)
to reflect the new structure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: decouple release build from release-please workflow

Replace the fragile workflow_call chain with an independent
release trigger. The release workflow now fires on the
'release: created' event, so it runs automatically when
Release Please creates a draft release + tag. This eliminates
the dependency on Release Please job outputs and avoids the
checkout failure when the tag doesn't match a branch name.

Changes:
- release.yml: trigger on release:created instead of workflow_call
- release-please.yml: remove build-release-assets downstream job
- Use env.RELEASE_TAG to resolve tag from either trigger type

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(terrain): correct chunk positioning, skirt stride, shader seaLevel

WorldManager.loadChunk:
- Remove overlap/UV extension (was causing floating slab seam artifacts)
- Geometry: PlaneGeometry(chunkSize) instead of chunkSize+16
- Mesh position: x*chunkSize + chunkSize/2 (correct centering)
- tHeightSize uniform: 513x513 (actual DataTexture size)

WorldManager._buildSkirt:
- ox/oz: cx*W instead of cx*W - W/2 (matches chunk geometry)
- Stride: TW = tileSize+1 = 513 (was using tileSize=512, reading wrong pixels)

terrain.vert.glsl:
- Remove obsolete R+G/255 precision packing (was for old PNG encoding)
- Add seaLevel uniform: vHeight = (h - seaLevel) * displacementScale
- Texel: 1/513 (matches actual DataTexture size)

terrain.frag.glsl:
- Add seaLevel uniform to getHeight() for correct normal calculation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Drop legacy connection support (#8883)

Replace all uses of `ActiveRecord::Base.connection` with
`ActiveRecord::Base.with_connection`, following Rails guidelines.

Direct connection usage is now dropped for Rails < 7.2, and
attempting to call .connection directly raises in Rails 8.x.
This aligns our codebase with current Rails recommendations and ensures
compatibility going forward.

Tests are updated to handle connection access correctly.
`with_connection` is designed to work correctly within transactional
tests. `quote_column_name` is a pure string operation with no DB
writes. No special transaction handling is required for these tests


Close #8881

References:
- Rails PR: https://github.com/rails/rails/pull/51349
- Connection Handling:
  https://github.com/rails/rails/blob/v7.2.1/activerecord/lib/active_record/connection_handling.rb#L259

Co-authored-by: tagliala <556268+tagliala@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: David Runger <davidjrunger@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Device fingerprint dedup for unique player tracking

Game (index.html):
- Generates device fingerprint from UA, language, screen, colorDepth,
  timezone, cores, touchpoints, platform  hashed to fp_XXXX
- Stored in 'theme' field of new_player analytics payload

Stats (stats.html):
- New '� New Players' tab with dedicated view
- Deduplicates by fingerprint: shows Unique Devices vs Total Signups
- Cards: unique, total, duplicates filtered, today, week, month
- Player table includes Device FP column
- Cloud tab now separates new_player entries from game sessions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Switch OneLake writer to Delta Lake format

Replaced raw Parquet file writes with deltalake library's
write_deltalake() for proper Delta table format. Data now
appears as managed tables in Fabric Lakehouse.

- Tables named {table_name}_{layer} (e.g. registration_intake_raw)
- Uses abfss:// URI with managed identity bearer token
- All columns as strings for schema flexibility
- Added ingested_at timestamp column
- Replaced pandas + azure-storage-file-datalake with deltalake

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix maintainerr errors (#46)

* Update to handle Maintainerr's API changes

* Update SDK to .NET 10 and update Packages (but not plex api :shame:)

* Add Claude cause why not. Lets see how he does

* Code review fixes: remove dead code, fix misleading attributes, harden error handling

- Remove commented-out PlexId from IMaintainerrMediaResponse
- Remove no-op [JsonPropertyName("plexId")] from computed MediaServerId properties in V2 models
- OverlayStateManager: throw ArgumentException on invalid MediaServerId instead of silently returning null or no-oping
- Remove stale root global.json (SDK 9.x); src/global.json governs all projects
- Change src/global.json rollForward from latestMajor to latestMinor for reproducibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update Dockerfile and CI workflows for .NET 10

- Bump runtime and SDK base images from 9.0 to 10.0
- Fix global.json COPY path in Dockerfile (root file deleted, now at src/global.json)
- Fix global-json-file path in build-test and release workflows for same reason

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
